title: CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE RE-ELECTED AS REGIONAL WHIP
text:
01.03.01
			 CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE RE-ELECTED AS REGIONAL WHIP 
			Washington, DC - Congresswoman Barbara Lee today was re-elected Whip of Region II by unanimous consent of her Northern California colleagues.The Regional Whips develop strategy, count votes, gather and distribute information among members from their region. Once  again, Congresswoman Lee will join Democratic Whip David Bonior in garnering support for key legislation and bringing members of the Democratic Caucus together in support of a strong Democratic agenda. "I look forward to working with my fellow Democrats to support a progressive legislative agenda that puts people first," said Lee. "Now more than ever we must be unified in putting forth an agenda that will protect Social Security and Medicare, strengthen public schools, and combat social and racial inequalities that still exist." Congresswoman Lee was elected Region II Whip in the 106th Congress, and was re-elected unanimously for her ability to bring colleagues to consensus on important legislation and Democratic initiatives.  "I am pleased that my Democratic colleagues have once again recognized the leadership of Rep. Barbara Lee by re-electing her to the important position of regional whip," said Rep. Nancy Pelosi (D-SF). "She distinguished herself in this position last session by lining up Democratic support for key party initiatives and we all look forward to her continued efforts in the new Congress." "I would like to thank my colleagues for their continued support and confidence, as well as the voters of California's Ninth Congressional District for entrusting me to be their voice in Congress," said Lee. "I look forward to the challenges and opportunities that we will face together in the 107th Congress." ###
	
	  

	
	
	
	
	
	
	

 
	    	  
		    
		    	Next Article

title: CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE AND CONGRESSIONAL BLACK CAUCUS OPPOSE FLORIDA ELECTORAL COLLEGE VOTES
text:
01.06.01
			 CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE AND CONGRESSIONAL BLACK CAUCUS OPPOSE FLORIDA ELECTORAL COLLEGE VOTES 
			Washington, DC - Congresswoman Barbara Lee today joined members of the Congressional Black Caucus in opposition to counting Florida's 25 electoral college votes for George W. Bush.formal objection to counting Florida=s electoral votes must be presented in writing, signed by at least one Senator and one Representative, under 3 U.S.C. section 15. Unfortunately, not one single Member of the Senate submitted an objection, thereby rendering the objection out of order. Rules may prevent the hearing of our challenge, but they do not relieve us of our responsibility to the people in this country who voted, only to find that their vote was not counted, said Lee. AWe will not stand silently by while African American voters are intimidated, dismissed from polling places, forced to use antiquated machines, and denied their rightful voice. The Congressional Black Caucus and civil rights organizations have pursued various initiatives and legal action in the months following the election. Congresswoman Lee, other members of the Congressional Black Caucus, and civil rights leaders met with Attorney General Janet Reno to request a Justice Department investigation into widespread voter intimidation and harassment, a disproportionate number of antiquated voting machines in minority communities, and other voting irregularities. Congresswoman Lee also recently co-sponsored H.R. 57, which would create a 12 member non-partisan commission to take a systemic look at federal election practices such as voter registration rules, ballot design, technology for counting ballots, and rules for mail-in and absentee ballots, poll locations, and a variety of other election reforms. I traveled to Florida to work to get out the vote for the same reason that I have served as an election observer abroad: because free and fair elections are the very lifeblood of a democracy and because the principle of one person, one vote must be more than empty rhetoric. said Lee. It is deplorable that many voters faced intimidation and barriers to casting their ballot for President, only to have their votes not counted, concluded Lee. ALet the world know that we failed in upholding our democratic principles and that the Reagan-Bush Supreme Court, not the people of the United States, decided the outcome of this election. We must challenge the acceptance of Presidential Electors from Florida, and take concerted action towards election reform that ensures that this never happens again. ###
	
	  

	
	
	
	
	
	
	

 
	    	  
		    
		    	Next Article

title: REPRESENTATIVE BARBARA LEE CALLS FOR ART SUBMISSIONS FOR U.S. CAPITOL EXHIBIT
text:
01.14.01
			 REPRESENTATIVE BARBARA LEE CALLS FOR ART SUBMISSIONS FOR U.S. CAPITOL EXHIBIT 
			Washington, DC - Congresswoman Barbara Lee today issued a call for submissions for the 20th Annual National High School art competition and exhibition called An Artistic Discovery, a year-long exhibit sponsored by more than 300 Members of Congress.High School students from the 9th Congressional District of California, which includes Alameda, Albany, Berkeley, Emeryville, Oakland and Piedmont, are requested to submit artwork to their high school principal's office. This art contest is very important for our community because it supports artistic expression and rewards high school students for their outstanding talent and accomplishments, said Lee. A We need to remember that activities such as art, music, and sports are essential to a student=s development and to our sense of identity, community, and pride. Each high school may establish their own deadline for the selection of no more than three works to be judged in the district-wide competition, but Congresswoman Lee's office must receive submissions by Friday, March 16, 2001. One work of art will be chosen by a panel of judges following a month-long exhibit in April, 2001, at the Junior Center for Arts and Sciences. The work of art chosen to represent the Ninth District of California will be displayed in the U.S. Capitol for one year. While only one student's artwork will be displayed in the U.S. Capitol, Congresswoman Lee pointed out the importance of displaying our students' work locally during the month of April. I am very pleased that, thanks to the collaborative efforts and support of several organizations and individuals, we will have an opportunity to celebrate and reward the artistic accomplishments of all our students. said Lee. The Junior Center is located at 558 Bellevue Avenue, Lake Merritt, in Oakland. It is open Monday through Friday from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. and on Saturdays from 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. The exhibit is free and open to the public. For more info contact Kimberlee Garfinkle, at Congresswoman Lees office, at (510) 763-0370 or visit the national competition=s website at www.house.gov/shadegg/artshow. ###
	
	  

	
	
	
	
	
	
	

 
	    	  
		    
		    	Next Article

title: CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE OPPOSES NOMINATION OF SENATOR JOHN ASHCROFT AS ATTORNEY GENERAL
text:
01.17.01
			 CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE OPPOSES NOMINATION OF SENATOR JOHN ASHCROFT AS ATTORNEY GENERAL 
			Oakland, CA - Congresswoman Barbara Lee today announced her opposition to the nomination of Senator John Ashcroft as Attorney General, while members of the Congressional Black Caucus walked out of the Senate nomination hearing in protest of his divisive record of public service."I am deeply troubled that Senator John Ashcroft has been nominated to lead the department that defends the rights and  freedoms of all Americans" said Lee. "He has built a career on opposing those rights and seeking to dismantle all that women  and minorities have fought for."  In December, Congresswoman Lee, other members of the Congressional Black Caucus, and civil rights leaders met with  Attorney General Janet Reno to request a Justice Department investigation into widespread voter intimidation and harassment, a disproportionate number of antiquated voting machines in minority communities, and other voting irregularities in Florida. The Congressional Black Caucus is concerned that the Justice Department will not aggressively pursue those investigations under Ashcroft, who has shown little commitment to protecting civil rights, including equal voting rights. "The one vote-one person principle must be more than empty rhetoric, and we must make sure that what happened in Florida  does not ever happen again," said Lee. "Ashcroft has shown little commitment to equal voting rights throughout his many years  in public service, and I am skeptical that he will pursue a course of action to remedy the voting rights violations that occurred in Florida."  "Furthermore, Senator Ashcroft has repeatedly tried to eliminate a woman's right to choose, and even opposes a woman's right to use common contraceptives such as birth control pills," said Lee. "He has opposed civil rights remedies, including school desegregation orders and affirmative action laws, and has failed to endorse racial profiling legislation. Ashcroft led an  unscrupulous campaign against a well qualified African American judge, and looks to the confederacy for his heroes."  Congresswoman Lee concluded, "By nominating a person with this kind of record to be the nation's top law enforcement official, President-elect Bush has dramatically widened the political rifts in our country; rifts he pledged to bridge during his campaign, and following his questionable election."   ###
	
	  

	
	
	
	
	
	
	

 
	    	  
		    
		    	Next Article

title: CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE TO BOYCOTT INAUGURATION OF PRESIDENT-ELECT BUSH
text:
01.19.01
			 CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE TO BOYCOTT INAUGURATION OF PRESIDENT-ELECT BUSH 
			Washington, DC - Congresswoman Barbara Lee today announced that she will not attend the inauguration of George W. Bush this Saturday, January 20, 2001."I cannot in good conscience celebrate the inauguration of George W. Bush when in Florida, thousands of Americans were  harassed, turned away, or otherwise disenfranchised in the November elections. This travesty of justice and democracy must  never happen again, and to that end, I have pledged to make the case at every appropriate opportunity that we need election  reform in this country, and we need it now! In losing the popular vote and being selected President by a U.S. Supreme Court ruling, President-elect Bush has no mandate  from the people. I challenge him to live up to his claim of being a unifier by embracing electoral reform legislation before  Congress and directing the U.S. Department of Justice to fully investigate voter disenfranchisement in the 2000 elections in  Florida. I look forward to working with President-elect Bush on this and other issues affecting the well-being of the American  people.  As the famous quote says, "Those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it." Too many have struggled and  died in this country for the right of all adult citizens to vote. We must not forget their fight and bloodshed. We must not forget  what happened in Florida in 2000. We must demand action now!"  ###
	
	  

	
	
	
	
	
	
	

 
	    	  
		    
		    	Next Article

title: CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE CRITICIZES PRESIDENT BUSH FOR RESTORING MEXICO CITY GAG RULE ON FAMILY PLANNING
text:
01.22.01
			 CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE CRITICIZES PRESIDENT BUSH FOR RESTORING MEXICO CITY GAG RULE ON FAMILY PLANNING 
			Washington, DC - Congresswoman Barbara Lee today voiced her strong disapproval of President Bush's decision to issue an executive order that restored the Mexico City Policy. The Mexico City Policy, created by President Reagan in 1984, strips non-governmental organizations that receive federal funding of their right to engage in a full range of family planning services in other countries. This policy was in effect until it was rescinded by President Clinton on January 22, 1993. "The decision of President Bush to reinstate the gag rule will not unify the country or work to close the partisan divide." said Lee. "By selecting anti-choice cabinet nominees like John Ashcroft and Tommy Thompson, and by reinstating the global 'gag' rule on the 28th anniversary of Roe v. Wade, President Bush has demonstrated that he will use his presidential powers to undermine the reproductive rights of the world's women." Approximately 600,000 women die each year of complications related to pregnancy and childbirth. Complications from pregnancy and childbirth are the leading cause of death and disability among women aged 15 and 49 in the developing countries.  The Fiscal Year 2001 Foreign Operations bill included $425 million for bilateral international family planning services and reproductive health education to families worldwide. This funding is now subject to the gag rule on abortion.  "It is crucial that women across the world have fundamental access to health care," said Lee. "This executive order restricts family planning organizations from even speaking about family planning options or lobbying their own government on reproductive health matters."  U.S. international family planning programs play a pivotal role in preventing unintended pregnancies and reducing the need for abortion worldwide. They are also crucial to preventing maternal deaths resulting from childbirth and stopping the spread of HIV/AIDS and sexually transmitted diseases. In many developing countries, international family planning programs represent the only health care available to women and their families. "Granting funds for family planning programs means saving lives, promoting women's and children's health, and strengthening families and communities around the world," said Lee. "This restriction would be unconstitutional if imposed here in the United  States, and should not imposed on organizations in other countries. Reinstating the global gag rule is extremely dangerous and  shortsighted." "I am deeply concerned that as we enter a new millennium, the Administration is seeking to return us to a period in our history when women did not have control over their own reproductive health," said Lee. "This demonstrates the President's commitment to eroding a woman's right to choose, and I fear that this is only the first wave of an assault on the reproductive freedoms in our own country. Now, more than ever, we must stand up and defend those freedoms that we have struggled so hard to attain." ###   
	
	  

	
	
	
	
	
	
	

 
	    	  
		    
		    	Next Article

title: CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE TO ATTEND UNITED NATIONS GENERAL ASSEMBLY SPECONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE INTRODUCES LEGISLATION FOR A NATIONAL AFFORDABLE HOUSING TRUST FUNDIAL SESSION ON HIV AND AIDS
text:
01.26.01
			 CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE TO ATTEND UNITED NATIONS GENERAL ASSEMBLY SPECONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE INTRODUCES LEGISLATION FOR A NATIONAL AFFORDABLE HOUSING TRUST FUNDIAL SESSION ON HIV AND AIDS 
			Washington, DC - Congresswoman Barbara Lee today introduced the National Affordable Trust Fund Act of 2001, tri-partisan
legislation that would use a portion of profit surpluses generated from the Federal Housing Administration (FHA) Mortgage
Insurance Program to create a trust fund to provide for the construction of affordable rental units and homes for low- and
middle-income Americans.  According to the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), over 5 million people are paying more than half of their
limited incomes on housing or living in severely substandard housing. In Oakland, only 45.8% of homes are affordable to those
making median incomes, earning the area a rank of 178th out of 193 metro areas surveyed the National Association of Home
Builders.  "This affordable housing crisis is real for the people in the Bay
Area and throughout the country," said Lee. There is more than  
   $2 billion in FHA and Ginnie Mae accounts that could be dedicated to addressing this issue, without jeopardizing these  
   programs."  The trust fund will use $2 billion in surplus Federal Housing
Administration funds, which are now lying dormant, to create a trust
fund from which states and non-profits could draw to build affordable
housing rental units, to construct affordable homes for low-
and middle-income individuals, and provide rental subsidies to
low-income individuals. This is a small portion of the more than
$26 billion profit expected to be generated by the FHA over the next
seven years.  "The rental vacancy rate for Oakland is 4.6%, well below the U.S.
average of 8.1%, and there are 3,000 families on waiting lists
for section 8 vouchers," said Lee. "Unfortunately, in most areas in the
region, the existing Section 8 subsidies no longer provide
a viable affordable housing option."  "These facts are sobering and even sad, but they are a reality for
most Californians, and for many people across the country,"  
   concluded Lee. "We need new and innovative ways to solve this severe lack of affordable housing, and the creation of the  
   National Housing Trust Fund will provide enormous resources to combat our housing crisis, without harming any of our existing
programs."  The tri-partisan National Housing Trust Fund Act is co-sponsored by Bernie Sanders (I-VT) and John McHugh (R-NY). It is  
   supported by the National Low Income Housing Coalition (NLIHC), the National Coalition for the Homeless (NCH), the Macauley
Institute, the Association of Community Organizations for Reform Now (ACORN) and other national housing organizations.
###
	
	  

	
	
	
	
	
	
	

 
	    	  
		    
		    	Next Article

title: CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE ELECTED VICE CHAIR OF CONGRESSIONAL PROGRESSIVE CAUCUS
text:
01.31.01
			 CONGRESSWOMAN BARBARA LEE ELECTED VICE CHAIR OF CONGRESSIONAL PROGRESSIVE CAUCUS 
			Washington, DC - Congresswoman Barbara Lee today was elected as Vice Chair of the Progressive Caucus.The more than 50 members of the Progressive Caucus share a common belief in the principles of social and economic justice, non-discrimination, and tolerance in America and in our relationships with other countries. The caucus seeks to embody and give a voice to national priorities which reflect the interests and needs of all the American people, not just the wealthy and the powerful. "I am very honored to be chosen as Vice Chair of the Progressive Caucus, and look forward to working with Chairman Dennis Kucinich of Ohio towards a progressive legislative agenda that puts people first," said Lee. "Now more than ever we must be unified in putting forth an agenda that will save Social Security and Medicare, increase access to health care for the more than 44 million Americans who are uninsured, provide affordable housing, strengthen public schools, protect the environment, and combat social and racial inequalities that still exist." "A majority of Americans agree with a progressive agenda that focuses on the needs and well being of all Americans,"said Lee. "We should not be afraid to differentiate our agenda from Republican initiatives which often-times ignore the working poor, low-income communities, as well as communities of color, instead pushing the agendas of the NRA, the Christian Coalition, defense contractors, anti-choice organizations, and the tobacco, pharmaceutical, insurance, and oil industries." Congresswoman Lee was elected Vice Chair of the Progressive Caucus because of a demonstrated record supporting peace and justice in the United States and throughout the world. "Barbara Lee is perhaps the most principled Member of Congress, with an understanding of the issues and a commitment to progressive change," said Progressive Caucus Chair Dennis Kucinich (D-OH) "Her new position reflects the confidence that her peers have in her, and she will do a tremendous job."  "I would like to thank my colleagues for their continued support and confidence, and for entrusting me with the responsibilities of this position in such an important caucus," said Lee. "I look forward to promoting an agenda of fairness and justice in the 107th Congress." ###
	
	  

	
	
	
	
	
	
	

 
	    	  
		    
		    	Next Article
